title: Event Settings
description: Configure your event details, visibility, distances, and registration settings.

wizard.title: Finish setting up your event
wizard.description: Add your first distance, then publish when you are ready to share.
wizard.stepZeroDescription: Choose how you want to set up this event first. You can switch paths at any time.

pathHint.ai: AI path selected. Use the assistant to draft updates, then continue editing manually as needed.
pathHint.manual: Manual path selected. Fill each section directly and switch to AI anytime.

issues.missingEventDate: Add an event start date to keep setup on track.
issues.missingEventLocation: Add an event location (city/state or map location).
issues.missingDistance: Add at least one distance for this edition.
issues.publishMissingDistance: Publish blocked: add at least one distance before publishing.
issues.publishMissingPricing: Publish blocked: each distance needs at least one price tier.
issues.recommendFaq: Recommended: add at least one FAQ item to answer common participant questions.
issues.recommendWaivers: Recommended: add a waiver so participants can acknowledge event terms.
issues.recommendQuestions: Recommended: add registration questions for logistics and participant preferences.
issues.recommendWebsite: Recommended: add website content so participants can review event details.
issues.recommendAddOns: Recommended: configure add-ons if you plan to offer extras.
issues.recommendPolicies: Recommended: review refund/transfer/deferral policies before publishing.

adaptive.title: Adaptive next steps
adaptive.description: Prioritized guidance from your current setup state.

wizard.assistantHint: Need help with setup? Use the assistant to draft suggested changes.

steps.distance: Add at least one distance with pricing.
steps.publish: Publish the event to make it visible.

wizardShell.title: Build your event step by step
wizardShell.description: Stay focused on the current step, move forward with confidence, and switch back to the full editor only when you choose to.

progress.label: Current progress
progress.blockersValue: {publish, plural, =0 {No publish blockers} one {# publish blocker} other {# publish blockers}}{required, plural, =0 {} one { · # required step still pending} other { · # required steps still pending}}
progress.skippedValue: {count, plural, =0 {No optional steps paused} one {# optional step paused} other {# optional steps paused}}

stepDescriptions.basics: Define the essential event identity, schedule, and location details that anchor the rest of setup.
stepDescriptions.distances: Create the distances runners can register for and configure how capacity should be managed.
stepDescriptions.pricing: Make sure every distance has a usable pricing structure before you move toward publishing.
stepDescriptions.registration: Set the registration window and control whether registrations are currently paused or active.
stepDescriptions.policies: Review organizer-facing policy settings and the participant waivers that will appear in registration.
stepDescriptions.content: Shape the participant-facing story with FAQ answers and rich website content.
stepDescriptions.extras: Add registration questions and optional extras only if they add real value for your event.
stepDescriptions.review: Check blockers, revisit skipped optional work, and control when the event becomes visible.

footer.description: This shell sets the foundation for the full numbered wizard. Later stories will wire each step body into this flow.
footer.optionalHint: You can complete this step now or skip it and revisit it later.
footer.ready: This step is in good shape. Continue when you're ready.

placeholder.description: {step} will be connected to its dedicated editor surface in the next wizard stories.

review.readyTitle: This event is ready for publish review
review.readyDescription: Required setup is complete. Review visibility and publish controls below when you are ready to make the event visible.
review.blockedDescription: {publish, plural, =0 {No publish blockers remain.} one {# publish blocker remains.} other {# publish blockers remain.}} {required, plural, =0 {} one {# setup requirement still needs attention.} other {# setup requirements still need attention.}}
review.serverAuthority: Final publish checks remain server-authoritative. The controls below confirm the real backend state before visibility changes.
review.blockersDescription: These items must be resolved before publish readiness can be trusted.
review.noBlockers: No blockers remain. The event is structurally ready for publish review.
review.recommendationsDescription: These items are optional, but they improve clarity and participant confidence.
review.skippedDescription: Optional steps you intentionally skipped stay visible here so they are easy to revisit.
review.noSkipped: No optional steps have been skipped.
review.skippedHelp: Skipped steps do not block publish, but they remain worth revisiting if you want a more complete participant experience.

assistant.title: Setup assistant
assistant.description: Describe your event in one message. I’ll propose changes you can review and apply.
assistant.contract: I only suggest updates. Nothing changes until you apply each proposal.

composer.sectionTitle: What do you want help with in this step?
composer.label: Message for setup assistant
composer.hint: Describe what you want for this step and I will propose reviewable changes. Rough notes are fine.
composer.briefHint: Use this box for what you want to solve right now. Rough notes are fine. Saved event notes stay above.
composer.savedBriefHint: The assistant already has your saved event notes. Add any rough extra details you want for this step here.
composer.roughNotesTitle: Paste rough notes. I will turn them into a clear proposal.
composer.roughNotesExample: Examples: "10K at night in Guadalajara, first-timers welcome, music at the finish" or "Need FAQ and site copy, but keep logistics grounded in confirmed details."

inline.title: AI assistant for {step}
inline.description: Get help for this step without leaving this setup flow.
inline.markdownHint: Use it to leave this section ready to publish.

mobile.stepHint: Get help with {step} without losing your place.

desktop.stepHint: Keep the editor comfortable and open a wider AI workspace for {step} only when you need it.
desktop.workspaceDescription: Use this expanded workspace to review proposals, refine prompts, and apply changes without squeezing the main editor.

latestProposal.title: Latest proposal
latestProposal.description: Review the newest AI recommendation first, then keep iterating from there.
latestProposal.supportingContextDescription: {count, plural, =0 {See the latest request and response if you need extra context.} one {See the latest request and response plus # earlier message.} other {See the latest request and response plus # earlier messages.}}

analyzing.title: Reviewing your request
analyzing.description: Checking your notes and the current {step} setup so the next proposal stays grounded.

grounding.title: Grounding the proposal
grounding.description: Cross-checking saved event details, existing setup, and open blockers before drafting {step}.

drafting.title: Drafting your next move
drafting.description: Shaping organizer-friendly guidance for {step} before the reviewable proposal is ready.

finalizing.title: Preparing the proposal card
finalizing.description: Packaging the final recommendation so you can review and apply it safely.

event_description.title: Starting with a grounded event description
event_description.description: I can shape the first useful draft around these pieces before the full proposal is ready.

sections.lead: A clear opening that frames the race in one confident paragraph.
sections.confirmed_highlights: Specific highlights pulled only from confirmed details and tone cues.
sections.pending_logistics: Logistics that should stay pending or omitted until they are confirmed.

faq.title: Starting with a usable FAQ structure
faq.description: I can draft the first answers faster by organizing the FAQ around the most likely participant questions.

sections.event_basics: What the event is, who it is for, and the essentials participants need first.
sections.route_and_distances: Grounded route or distance details that are already confirmed.
sections.registration_and_logistics: Registration, timing, and logistics only where the current setup supports them.

website_overview.title: Starting with a website overview scaffold
website_overview.description: I can move faster by framing the site copy around the confirmed event story before expanding it.

sections.hero_positioning: A grounded hero message that sets the tone without overpromising.
sections.confirmed_experience: Specific event qualities or race-day feel that the snapshot already supports.
sections.what_to_confirm: Items to keep tentative until the organizer confirms them.

policy.title: Starting with a policy draft structure
policy.description: I can prepare a clearer first draft by organizing the policy into the sections participants expect.

sections.core_rule: The main rule or commitment participants need to understand first.
sections.participant_responsibility: Participant-facing responsibilities or acknowledgements stated plainly.
sections.open_logistics: Operational details that should remain open until they are confirmed.

basics.title: I already know how to start this basics draft
basics.description: While I finish the final proposal, I can keep the work grounded around these basics decisions.

sections.first_pass: Start with identity and positioning updates that do not require inventing date or venue details.
sections.confirmed_facts: Reuse only confirmed basics such as the current title, location, timezone, and saved organizer brief.
sections.open_points: Leave schedule and exact venue logistics pending if they are still unconfirmed.

content.title: I already have a content draft plan forming
content.description: The final proposal is still coming, but this is the structure I am using to keep it useful and publishable.

sections.first_pass: Draft the most useful participant-facing section first instead of waiting for every detail.
sections.confirmed_facts: Anchor the copy in confirmed event details, saved notes, and localized content already on the event.
sections.open_points: Keep missing logistics clearly pending instead of filling them with generic promises.

policies.title: I am structuring the policy draft already
policies.description: Before the final proposal arrives, this is how I am keeping the policy readable and grounded.

sections.first_pass: Start with the clearest participant-facing rule or waiver structure first.
sections.confirmed_facts: Use only the rules, constraints, and event context that are already confirmed.
sections.open_points: Leave operational details open if the organizer has not confirmed them yet.

review.title: I am organizing the next publish-ready move
review.description: The final proposal is still being assembled, but this is the shape I am using to keep the review helpful.

sections.first_pass: Prioritize the most useful blocker fix or participant-copy improvement first.
sections.confirmed_facts: Base the recommendation on current blockers, saved notes, and existing event content.
sections.open_points: Keep unresolved logistics visible instead of hiding them behind polished filler.

generic.title: I am turning this into a grounded first pass
generic.description: The final proposal is still loading, but the response is already being organized around safe, useful value.

sections.first_pass: Start with the smallest useful draft the current step can support safely.
sections.confirmed_facts: Lean on confirmed event details and saved organizer context before making claims.
sections.open_points: Keep unknowns visible so the proposal stays honest and reviewable.

slow.title: Still shaping a grounded proposal
slow.description: This request needs a little more time because I am cross-checking your event details before I suggest anything.

verySlow.title: Taking extra care with the final proposal
verySlow.description: I am still working through your notes and confirmed event data so the recommendation stays useful and safe to apply.

archive.title: Earlier conversation
archive.description: {count, plural, one {# earlier message} other {# earlier messages}}

continuity.title: I am still carrying your latest proposal from {step}
continuity.description: If you moved here from a suggestion, the last useful exchange stays visible while you continue.

basics.draftFromBrief: Use my rough notes to start this event.
basics.suggestStrongerPositioning: Make the title and summary feel stronger.
basics.reviewMostImportantMissingDetails: Tell me the most important basics still missing.

distances.createDistanceLineup: Use my notes to suggest the distance lineup.
distances.suggestCommonDistanceSet: What distance mix would fit this event best?

pricing.draftPricingStructure: Suggest realistic pricing for this event.
pricing.proposeMarketReadyPricing: Suggest prices that feel realistic for this event.
pricing.fixNextPricingBlocker: Tell me which pricing blocker to fix next.

policies.writeClearWaiverCopy: Write a waiver that feels easy to understand.
policies.improvePolicyClarity: Review this step and improve the biggest clarity issue.

content.improveContentClarity: Make it clearer and more trustworthy.

review.explainCurrentBlockers: Explain what is still blocking publish.
review.fixNextRequiredStep: Help me with the next required step.

brief.description: Key context the assistant can keep using across the wizard.
brief.emptyHint: Save the event vision, audience, tone, and must-haves once so the assistant can build from them later.
brief.inputHint: Edit the event notes you want the assistant to keep using throughout setup.
brief.useForStepPrompt: Use the event notes you already have and help me with {step}.

assistant.placeholder: Tell me what you want to improve in this step...

empty.heading: Rough notes are enough to start.
empty.example: For example: evening 10K in Guadalajara, friendly vibe, live music at the finish, help me write the event summary and tell me what still matters most.

errors.title: Assistant error
errors.proRequired: Setup assistant is a Pro feature.
errors.readOnlyTitle: Assistant is read-only for this membership
errors.readOnlyDescription: You can keep working through the wizard, but only organizers with edit access can use or apply assistant proposals.
errors.rateLimited: Too many assistant requests. Please wait a moment and try again.
errors.requestFailedHint: Something went wrong with the assistant request. Please try again.
errors.invalid: That proposal could not be applied as written. Review the step details and try again.
errors.retryLater: The assistant could not apply that proposal right now. Please try again in a moment.

safety.promptInjection: That request looks unrelated to event setup. Try asking for help with this step.
safety.policyViolation: That request can't be handled here. Try a more specific event-setup request.

errors.partialApplied: Some changes were applied, but something failed. Please review your event settings.
errors.failed: Could not apply changes. Please try again.

visibility.title: Event Visibility
visibility.description: Control who can see and access your event. Published events appear in the public directory.

errors.missingDistances: Add at least one distance before publishing.
errors.missingPrices: Add at least one price for every distance before publishing.
errors.generic: Unable to update visibility. Please try again.

registration.title: Registration Status
registration.description: Pause or resume registration for this event. Paused events won't accept new registrations.

heroImage.title: Hero image
heroImage.description: Show a wide image on your event page and directory card.

errors.invalidType: Please upload a JPG, PNG, WebP, or GIF.
errors.saveFailed: Could not save the hero image.

capacity.title: Capacity settings
capacity.description: Choose whether capacity is per distance or shared across the event.
capacity.toggleHelp: When enabled, all distances pull from one shared pool.
capacity.sharedCapacityHelp: Total registrants allowed across all distances.

errors.required: Enter a total capacity to enable shared pool mode.

descriptionField.label: Event description
descriptionField.help: Give participants a clear overview of the event.

details.title: Event Details
details.slugRedirectInfo: Old edition links are automatically forwarded to the new URL.

slugConfirm.title: Update edition URL?
slugConfirm.description: We’ll automatically forward your old edition links to the new URL, so existing shares and marketing keep working.

distances.title: Distances
distances.empty: No distances added yet. Add your first distance to enable registration.
distances.confirmDelete: Are you sure you want to delete this distance? This action cannot be undone.
